humanSummary.text: UTSUSHI-148 delivers the planned tightening of the browser-launch contract for MV/MZ alpha. The `BrowserLaunchAdapter` host-availability diagnostic is now severity `error` (crates/utsushi-fixture/src/launch_adapters.rs:289-307), `resolve_browser_program` returns a typed `RuntimeHarnessError` whose kind is one of the new `ChromiumUnavailable` / `ChromiumVersionMismatch` / `ChromiumDisplayUnavailable` variants in utsushi-core (lib.rs:2889-2929), and `BrowserUnavailabilityReason::semantic_code` maps the three production-reachable reasons to `utsushi.browser.chromium_unavailable`, `utsushi.browser.chromium_version_mismatch`, and (gated-off / test-only via `force_display_unavailable`) `utsushi.browser.display_unavailable`. The forbidden-optionality grep in launch_adapters.rs returns the two literal matches at lines 1541-1542, both inside the negative-assertion test `browser_descriptor_omits_when_host_support_exists_optionality_language`, confirming the test specifies that the descriptor JSON does NOT carry that language. `NwjsLaunchAdapter::descriptor` advertises an empty `capabilities: vec![]`, a `research_tier_status` `info`-severity diagnostic (status `unsupported`, `errorCode = utsushi.runtime.research_tier_unsupported`, `runtimeTier = research`, `supersededBy = utsushi-browser`), and a first-limitation string that contains both `research-tier` and `not advertised as an alpha capability`; the trace/capture/smoke_validate methods all return a `RuntimeHarnessError` with `kind = ResearchTierUnsupported`, code `runtime_research_tier_unsupported`, and detail `semanticCode = utsushi.runtime.research_tier_unsupported` (launch_adapters.rs:925-994). The broken-env test scopes its `unsafe { env::set_var }` inside an `EnvGuard` struct that captures the prior value in `set()` and restores or removes it on `Drop` (launch_adapters.rs:1850-1875), satisfying the audit ask. Engine-neutral semantic codes are observed: a grep for rpg/maker/mv/mz inside `crates/utsushi-fixture/src/launch_adapters.rs` and `crates/utsushi-core/src/lib.rs` finds only descriptor-text references that explain WHO requires Chromium, never inside the semantic-code namespace. Cargo-side verification reruns from the worktree match the worker's report: `cargo test -p utsushi-fixture` 34 passed (including all 14 new launch_adapter tests), `cargo test -p utsushi-core` 117 passed across binaries, `cargo test -p utsushi-cli` 11 passed (including the three new/updated capability tests), `cargo fmt --check` clean, `cargo check --workspace` clean, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-fixture --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean. However: `just check` FAILS from the worktree (recipe `check` exit 1) because `pnpm exec vp check` reports markdown formatting issues in two files the worker modified — `.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md` and `docs/alpha-localization-project-readiness.md`. Running `vp check --fix` produces an exact, scoped diff: (a) blank line inserted before a list (.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md:291), (b) padded markdown-table column widths in the semantic-code catalog table and the alpha role table, (c) blockquote continuation lines need leading `>` (.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md:583), (d) escape `*` as `\*` in `utsushi.browser.\*` to keep markdown emphasis from triggering, (e) a stray indented bullet continuation (.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md:642, :683). All five are cosmetic-only and do not change committed prose. Per the audit instructions, `just check` failure is a P1 finding (UTSUSHI-148-F001). `just test` initially fails for an unrelated environmental reason (`@itotori/localization-bridge-schema` / `@itotori/db` ts:test consumers cannot resolve the schema package until its dist is built); after running `pnpm --filter @itotori/localization-bridge-schema build && pnpm --filter @itotori/db build` once, `just test` passes (vitest: 14+19 test files passed, cargo workspace tests all green). This is a pre-existing devshell prerequisite, not a regression from this work, but is recorded as P3 follow-up (UTSUSHI-148-F003) because the worker's `just test` claim in the report was 'cargo-side gates verified' only — a `just test` lane on a clean worktree must self-bootstrap. Acceptance criteria: (1) Chromium launch is required for MV/MZ alpha — descriptor's Launch feature description and contract limitations both declare `Required for MV/MZ alpha runtime evidence`, plus enforced by tests `browser_descriptor_capability_contract_marks_browser_launch_as_required_for_mv_mz_alpha` and `browser_descriptor_omits_when_host_support_exists_optionality_language`; (2) environmental misconfiguration is a hard error — `resolve_browser_program` returns typed errors not `Ok(_)`, severity is `error`, four broken-env paths covered by tests (`browser_run_returns_chromium_unavailable_kind_when_binary_missing`, `browser_run_returns_chromium_unavailable_when_env_browser_bin_broken`, `browser_run_returns_chromium_version_mismatch_when_version_too_old`, `reserved_display_unavailable_reason_carries_typed_semantic_code`); (3) NW.js demoted to research-tier — capabilities vector is empty, `research_tier_status` diagnostic present at info severity, all three runtime operations return `ResearchTierUnsupported`; (4) no `when host support exists` optionality — guarded by descriptor-output regression test. No P0/P1 architectural findings. One P1 verification finding (just check formatting). Three follow-ups: F002 (`--skip-browser` CI recipe shape — worker pre-identified P2), F003 (strict display checking follow-up — worker pre-identified P2; reserved variant wired and tested but not produced in production paths), F004 (Cargo-side detection refactor candidate: probe is re-invoked on every `descriptor()` call AND every `resolve_browser_program()` call, meaning each descriptor render runs `<binary> --version` synchronously up to the 2s timeout — a minor inefficiency / observable side-effect not violating the contract but worth a P3 cache pass).

findings.title: `just check` fails on markdown formatting in worker-modified .plan and docs files
findings.description: Re-running `just check` from the worktree fails with exit code 1: `pnpm exec vp check` reports formatting issues in `.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md` and `docs/alpha-localization-project-readiness.md`, both files the worker modified or created in this DAG node. Running `vp check --fix` produces a scoped, cosmetic-only diff: (a) blank line required before a list, (b) markdown-table column widths padded so all rows align (semantic-code catalog and the MV/MZ alpha role table), (c) blockquote continuation lines need leading `>`, (d) `*` in `utsushi.browser.*` escaped as `\*` so markdown emphasis is not triggered, (e) two stray indented bullet continuations. The audit instructions explicitly state: 'If `just check` or `just test` fails, that's a P1.' The worker pre-noted that they could not run `just check` in their sandbox (no offline pnpm/vp install), which is consistent with this slipping through; nothing in their commits is logically wrong, but the verification gate is red on the as-committed state.

evidence.detail: `cd /scratch/worktrees/itotori-spec-utsushi-148 && direnv exec /home/trevor/projects/itotori just check` exits 1: `pnpm exec vp check` -> 'Formatting issues found .plan/UTSUSHI-148.md docs/alpha-localization-project-readiness.md / Found formatting issues in 2 files (1264ms, 48 threads). Run `vp check --fix` to fix them. / error: recipe `check` failed on line 14 with exit code 1'.

`pnpm exec vp check --fix` succeeds and the resulting `git diff --stat` is: `.plan/UTSUSHI-148.md | 21 +++++++++++---------- docs/alpha-localization-project-readiness.md | 16 ++++++++--------`. All changes are cosmetic table alignment, blank-line-before-list, escaped `*`, and blockquote continuation markers — no prose semantics change.

Cargo gates (`cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --workspace`, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-fixture --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`) are all clean — the failure is markdown-only.

findings.impact: Blocks completion: the DAG completion criterion that re-running `just check` from the worktree succeeds is currently red. Mechanical fix; no architectural rework required. Once `vp check --fix` is run and committed, this finding clears and the rest of the work should be acceptable.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Run `pnpm exec vp check --fix` from the worktree (after `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`) so the two affected files conform to the workspace markdown formatter.
Confirm the resulting diff is cosmetic-only (no prose-level changes) and commit as an additive fix on top of 05d241f.
Re-run `just check` from the worktree and confirm exit 0.

findings.title: Public CI `--skip-browser` recipe shape: declared at recipe layer but no implementation lands here
findings.description: The updated docs commit (a526ce9) reads: 'Public CI that intentionally lacks Chromium must declare the skip at the recipe layer; the adapter itself does not silently degrade.' This is the correct architectural posture for UTSUSHI-148 (no in-adapter optionality), but no recipe-layer skip mechanism is implemented or wired into any concrete CI lane in this commit set. Worker pre-identified this as P2 in the report-back and confirmed 'no CI lane currently gates on browser severity == error' so no immediate fallout. Recording as a follow-up so when the first Chromium-less public CI lane is introduced (Linux CI containers without Chromium, or a deliberately bounded CI runner) the recipe-layer skip is designed and shipped before that lane goes green.

evidence.detail: Docs commit a526ce9 declares the recipe-layer skip as the operator's responsibility but does not point to an implementation.
Worker pre-identified this candidate as P2 in their report-back and confirmed no CI lane currently gates on browser severity.

findings.impact: Once a CI lane that lacks Chromium starts consuming the capability output, today's UTSUSHI-148 behaviour (severity = error, hard runtime failure on launch) becomes a CI-red event. The recipe-layer skip is the orchestrator-approved escape valve but is not implemented. Mid-priority because no such CI lane exists today.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Define the `--skip-browser` (or equivalently named) flag on the `utsushi capabilities` or `utsushi smoke` CLI surface that an operator can pass to acknowledge a Chromium-less environment.
Make the flag explicit (no env-driven implicit skip) and have it emit a clearly labelled `runtime_skip_acknowledged` diagnostic so the report-side cannot be mistaken for a passing alpha claim.
Document in `docs/subprojects-utsushi.md` exactly which CI lanes are allowed to pass the flag and which are forbidden (MV/MZ alpha claim lanes MUST NOT).

proposedDagNode.title: Recipe-layer `--skip-browser` flag for Chromium-less CI lanes
proposedDagNode.summary: Implement the recipe-layer skip mechanism the UTSUSHI-148 docs declare as the operator's escape valve for Chromium-less public CI lanes, so the in-adapter `severity = error` posture stays intact while CI runners that intentionally lack Chromium can pass.
deliverables: `--skip-browser` (or equivalently named) CLI flag on capabilities/smoke surfaces
Typed `runtime_skip_acknowledged` diagnostic emitted when the flag is set so the report cannot be mistaken for an alpha pass
Docs update enumerating which CI lanes are allowed to pass the flag
acceptanceCriteria: Flag is explicit (no env-driven implicit skip).
MV/MZ alpha-claim lanes are documented as forbidden from passing the flag.
Capability report status under the flag is distinguishable from a successful Chromium probe.
auditFocus: Skip mechanism must not silently degrade alpha-claim lanes
Diagnostic shape distinguishes skip from success
Documentation enumerates allowed and forbidden CI lanes

findings.title: Strict display checking: reserved BrowserUnavailabilityReason::DisplayUnavailable variant is wired but never produced in production paths
findings.description: The `BrowserUnavailabilityReason::DisplayUnavailable` variant carries `#[allow(dead_code)]` because its production producer (a strict display/DRI probe) is intentionally not implemented in UTSUSHI-148. The wiring (semantic code mapping to `utsushi.browser.display_unavailable`, harness error kind mapping to `ChromiumDisplayUnavailable`, diagnostic detail attachment, harness-error detail attachment) IS live and exercised through the `force_display_unavailable()` test helper and the test `reserved_display_unavailable_reason_carries_typed_semantic_code`. Worker pre-identified this as P2. The fact that the strict-display-probe gate is reserved is consistent with the plan; the follow-up is the production gate. The reserved variant carries `platform: &'static str` and a `DisplayProbeOutcome` that distinguishes `PresentEnv`, `HeadlessOnly`, and `UnavailableStrict`, but the production code path (`probe_chromium`) currently never returns this variant — `--headless=new --disable-gpu --no-sandbox` is hard-coded into launch args so the adapter operates headlessly regardless of DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY env vars.

evidence.detail: Reserved variant declared with `#[allow(dead_code)]` and a doc-comment explaining that production paths do not produce it.

`reserved_display_unavailable_reason_carries_typed_semantic_code` pins the semantic code, harness error kind, and detail-attachment wiring against `force_display_unavailable()`. The production code path (`probe_chromium`) never returns this variant.

findings.impact: Low/medium: the reserved variant is wired so a follow-up that flips the production gate inherits a working contract, which is exactly the right posture. The strict-display gate itself is not in scope for UTSUSHI-148 alpha. Mid-priority because alpha-environment operators who set up a CI runner with a hardware decoder but a broken DISPLAY may want a structured diagnostic instead of an opaque Chromium launch failure.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Decide the activation policy for strict display checking (env-var-gated? always-on under non-Linux? operator-explicit?).
Implement the production producer that returns `BrowserUnavailabilityReason::DisplayUnavailable { platform, probe }` when the gate fires.
Remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotation and the test-only `force_display_unavailable` shim.
Add an integration test that covers the production path under the chosen gate.

proposedDagNode.title: Strict display checking: produce DisplayUnavailable variant in production
proposedDagNode.summary: Promote the reserved `BrowserUnavailabilityReason::DisplayUnavailable` variant from test-only to a real strict-display probe, so operators of CI runners with broken display surfaces get a typed `utsushi.browser.display_unavailable` outcome instead of an opaque Chromium launch failure.
deliverables: Activation policy for strict display checking documented and chosen
Production producer for the DisplayUnavailable variant
Removal of `#[allow(dead_code)]` and the `force_display_unavailable` test shim
Integration test for the production path
acceptanceCriteria: Reserved variant is produced from real probe code under the chosen activation gate.
Existing UTSUSHI-148 tests continue to pass.
Typed semantic code `utsushi.browser.display_unavailable` is observable in capability output under the gate.
auditFocus: Gate activation policy must not regress existing CI lanes
Production producer is reachable under realistic broken-display conditions
Operator-facing message distinguishes display-broken from binary-missing

findings.title: Chromium probe runs synchronously on every descriptor() and every launch with no caching
findings.description: `BrowserLaunchAdapter::probe()` is invoked from `browser_host_availability_diagnostic()`, `descriptor_limitations()`, AND `resolve_browser_program()` — i.e. every `descriptor()` call triggers TWO synchronous probes (each running `<binary> --version` with a 2-second hard timeout) and every launch triggers a third. The inline rationale (lines 263-266: 'The probe is intentionally invoked on every descriptor render and on every launch so the diagnostic reflects fresh host state (environment can change between capability listing and launch)') is defensible — staleness is real — but the cost is observable: the bounded-timeout regression test `browser_descriptor_version_probe_is_bounded_by_timeout` allows up to 5 seconds elapsed per descriptor render against a wedged probe, and a host with a slow-spawning Chromium will pay that cost on every CLI invocation that reads the capability surface. Not a correctness issue.

evidence.detail: Inline rationale acknowledges fresh-state re-probe is by design.
Both descriptor helpers call `self.probe()` independently, so `descriptor()` triggers two probes per call.

findings.impact: Low: hosts with a working Chromium see two extra ~tens-of-ms `--version` invocations per descriptor render. Worst case is a wedged binary that takes 2s each (4s per descriptor). No correctness gap; the architectural posture (no stale-data risk) is the right default. A simple per-call cache (probe once, share both diagnostic and limitations) would halve the cost without compromising freshness.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Refactor `descriptor()` to call `self.probe()` once and pass the result into both `browser_host_availability_diagnostic` and `descriptor_limitations` (e.g. helpers that take `&Result<ChromiumProbeOutcome, BrowserUnavailabilityReason>`).
Keep `resolve_browser_program()` as a separate, independent probe — staleness between capability render and launch is the intentional design.

Regression test that asserts `descriptor()` invokes `<binary> --version` at most once per call (mirror of the existing `browser_descriptor_does_not_invoke_browser_launch_during_version_probe` pattern).

proposedDagNode.title: Cache Chromium probe within a single descriptor() call
proposedDagNode.summary: Share the bounded Chromium probe outcome within a single `BrowserLaunchAdapter::descriptor()` call so a descriptor render runs at most one `<binary> --version` instead of two, while keeping the per-launch re-probe (intentional fresh-state check).
deliverables: Refactored `descriptor()` that calls `probe()` once and threads the result into diagnostic and limitations helpers
Regression test asserting at-most-one --version invocation per descriptor render
acceptanceCriteria: Existing UTSUSHI-148 tests continue to pass.
Probe staleness between capability render and launch is preserved (launch path still re-probes).
Worst-case wedged-binary descriptor cost halves from 4s to 2s.
auditFocus: Cache is per-call, not per-adapter (no Mutex<state>)
Test coverage of the at-most-one assertion

findings.title: `just test` requires manual pnpm schema/db build prerequisites on a clean worktree
findings.description: On a clean worktree, `just test` fails because vitest cannot resolve `@itotori/localization-bridge-schema` — its `dist/` is not present until `pnpm --filter @itotori/localization-bridge-schema build && pnpm --filter @itotori/db build` is run. After the two builds, `just test` passes cleanly (vitest: 14 + 19 test files passed, cargo workspace tests all green, 0 failures). This is pre-existing, NOT a regression from UTSUSHI-148, but I am recording it because the audit instruction is explicit that `just test` must succeed from the worktree, and the as-shipped `just test` recipe does not self-bootstrap. Worker's report-back said cargo-side gates were verified and `just test` was not runnable in their sandbox, which is consistent with this.

evidence.detail: `just test` from a clean worktree fails: vitest -> 'Error: Failed to resolve entry for package "@itotori/localization-bridge-schema". The package may have incorrect main/module/exports specified in its package.json.'
After `pnpm --filter @itotori/localization-bridge-schema build && pnpm --filter @itotori/db build`, `just test` passes: vitest test files 14+19 passed, cargo workspace tests all green.
`just test` recipe runs `pnpm exec vp run ts:test && cargo test --workspace` with no upstream build step.

findings.impact: Low: a developer running `just test` after a fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` hits a confusing TS-module-resolution error instead of a clear build-first instruction. Not specific to UTSUSHI-148 and does not affect any correctness claim. Recording as P3 because the audit instructions demanded a worktree re-run.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Add a prerequisite recipe (e.g. `just itotori-scale-build` or `just install && just <schema-build>`) before `pnpm exec vp run ts:test` in the `test` recipe, OR document the bootstrap step in `README.md` and/or `docs/subprojects-utsushi.md`.
Alternative: have vitest configs point at the source `src/` of `@itotori/localization-bridge-schema` and `@itotori/db` instead of `dist/` so vitest can resolve without a build step.

proposedDagNode.title: `just test` self-bootstrap on a clean worktree
proposedDagNode.summary: Make `just test` succeed on a freshly-installed worktree without requiring a manual `pnpm --filter @itotori/localization-bridge-schema build && pnpm --filter @itotori/db build` first.
deliverables: `just test` recipe self-bootstraps prerequisite TS package builds, OR vitest configs resolve to `src/` instead of `dist/`
acceptanceCriteria: `just test` succeeds on a clean worktree after only `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
No regression in CI test lanes.
auditFocus: No double-build cost in the developer loop
